props.message:
The path forks here.

East lies a hollow under a fallen elder. North, the path runs to the bridge.

Hollow of the Last Wyrd.

A carved root frames the entrance. The dust inside is fresh.

A frayed ribbon, knotted to a low branch.

Someone marked this place. You feel the Source listening more closely here.

props.open_message: A handful of river-pebbles, perfect for the sling.

root.speaker: Hermit Olber
root.text: Eh? Is it morning? Is it night? The wood does not say. I have walked in circles for — well. Some time. You look less lost than I am. Tell me, are you looking for the Hollow?

hollow.speaker: Hermit Olber
hollow.text: East. East, then south. There is a fallen elder-tree, its roots carved like a mouth. The mouth swallows the path. That is the entrance. I would go in, but the door has no handle, and I have no patience.

wander.speaker: Hermit Olber
wander.text: Long enough to forget my own name some mornings. The wood folds upon itself, you see. The ribbons others have tied — those are the only true compass.

root.speaker: Wenna the Wisp-tender
root.text:
Hush — they are timid. *cups her hands gently*
Good. Good. I see your wisp at your shoulder, traveler. She is bright. She has chosen you.

role.speaker: Wenna the Wisp-tender
role.text: A wisp is a fleck of the Source with a small, bright will. She lights what is dim. She remembers what you forget. She names what you cannot. Listen for her chime — it speaks where words will not.

choose.speaker: Wenna the Wisp-tender
choose.text: Because the Source likes a true heart, and a true heart never knows itself. Glim sees in you what you cannot. Trust her sight more than your own, when the green grows thick.

bye.speaker: Wenna the Wisp-tender
bye.text: Walk lightly. The wisps sleep nearby.
